VAL-CROSS-002.evidence: Miri analysis (M2, commit 8441e45) found zero kernel UB — all findings were expected bare-metal patterns (integer-to-pointer casts in heap tests) or out-of-scope (unaligned AtomicU32 in IPC library, not kernel). Since no actionable UB patterns were discovered by Miri, the 'If Miri found UB patterns' condition is vacuously satisfied. Additionally, Miri's documented assembly-dependent blind spots (TLB invalidation, break-before-make, address translation) were independently covered by the 6-category AArch64 correctness checklist applied during M3-M4 audits. Specifically: memory.rs AUDIT comment confirms 'Break-before-make correct' and 'TLB sequences correct per ARM ARM D5.10.2', directly addressing the TLB blind spot Miri documented. The Miri compatibility doc (.factory/library/miri-compatibility.md) is comprehensive: 685/965 tests pass, 26 ignored with reasons, 2 binaries skipped (timeout), full assembly blind spot analysis with risk ratings.
VAL-CROSS-002.details: Evidence chain: (1) miri-compatibility.md states 'Zero kernel UB found' with per-file results table showing 685/965 pass, 26 ignored, 0 UB. (2) Assembly blind spots section documents 7 categories of asm-dependent code Miri cannot analyze. (3) M3-M4 audit AUDIT comments in heap.rs, slab.rs, page_allocator.rs, memory.rs, memory_region.rs, paging.rs, memory_mapped_io.rs all apply the 6-category checklist including AArch64 correctness — covering the same areas Miri flags as blind spots. (4) Git log confirms Miri analysis (commit 8441e45) preceded allocator audits (commits 2718ab3-d203ce2) and memory audits (commits 56f8573-364c412), establishing correct workflow ordering.

VAL-CROSS-003.evidence: Strong cross-pollination from audit findings to adversarial tests. Adversarial test files explicitly cite audit milestones as their targets. Specific mappings: (1) heap.rs audit found 'dealloc routing invariant (address-based)' → heap_routing.rs tests this exact invariant with greedy slab stub. (2) page_allocator.rs audit found 'PA validation on free (page-aligned, within RAM range)' → adversarial_buddy.rs header says 'Targets findings from the memory-paging-pagealloc audit (milestone 1)'. (3) slab.rs audit found 'alignment correct: obj_size power-of-two ≥ 64' → adversarial_slab.rs fuzzes size_class with all sizes 0-4200+ and 12 alignment values, header says 'Targets allocator routing correctness from the allocator-mmio audit'. (4) memory_region.rs audit found 'boundary addresses (0, u64::MAX-PAGE_SIZE)' → adversarial_vma.rs exercises boundary addresses, header says 'Targets findings from the memory audit and memory_region.rs review'. (5) heap.rs audit documented OOM behavior → oom.rs tests OOM fault injection paths. (6) adversarial_stress.rs header explicitly states 'targeting specific audit findings from milestones 1-6' with per-section citations: milestone 4 channel-handle audit, milestone 3 scheduler-algorithm audit (vruntime overflow), milestone 4 sync-primitives audit, milestone 5 interrupt-timer audit (saturating_add fix), milestone 2 process-lifecycle audit, milestone 6 cross-module-lifetime audit.
VAL-CROSS-003.details: Evidence chain: (1) adversarial_buddy.rs line 4-5: 'Targets findings from the memory-paging-pagealloc audit (milestone 1)'. (2) adversarial_slab.rs line 5: 'Targets the allocator routing correctness from the allocator-mmio audit (milestone 1)'. (3) adversarial_vma.rs lines 4-5: 'Targets findings from the memory audit (milestone 1) and memory_region.rs review'. (4) adversarial_stress.rs lines 1-2: 'Adversarial stress / fuzz tests targeting specific audit findings from milestones 1-6', with 10+ section headers citing specific milestone audits. (5) heap_routing.rs tests the dealloc-by-address invariant documented in heap.rs AUDIT comment. (6) oom.rs tests the OOM/exhaustion paths documented in heap.rs AUDIT comment. (7) Git log shows audit commits (2718ab3 through 364c412) preceded adversarial test commits (c168f20 through bfa0a3c), confirming audit→fuzz workflow.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: both pass. VAL-CROSS-002 passes because Miri found zero kernel UB (vacuously satisfied conditional), and Miri's assembly blind spots were independently covered by the AArch64 correctness category in M3-M4 audits. VAL-CROSS-003 passes with strong evidence: all 7 adversarial test files explicitly cite audit milestones as their design targets, and specific audit findings (dealloc routing, PA validation, slab alignment, VMA boundaries, OOM handling) have corresponding dedicated tests.
